rationale: The citing paper gives Lee (2002) a distinct conceptual role in its theory section, not just a background list mention. It explicitly summarizes Lee's finding that perceptual processing is more influential in stimulus-based tasks while conceptual processing is more influential in memory-based tasks, and uses this as evidence for its core mechanism. That mechanism directly supports the paper's main hypothesis that left-digit bias is stronger under stimulus-based than memory-based price evaluations. Because this prior work helps ground the central framing and hypothesis logic, the citation is substantive and foundational.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Lee (2002) as key prior evidence that evaluation mode shifts reliance between perceptual and conceptual representations. It builds its left-digit-bias mechanism on this distinction when developing its main hypotheses.
